litmus_result: PASS: NULL run reward 0 with all 5 f2p reporting per-test FAILED (no collection errors); ORACLE run (solve.sh) reward 1 with 17/17 required passing; PARTIAL run (2 of 5 defects reversed: D1+D4) reward 0 with 3 f2p still FAILED. git log --oneline == 1 line, git status clean, git diff empty in the built task image.
base_defects_visible_suite: GREEN (the repo ships no test files; the agent-visible tree has nothing to run and no failing local test points at any defect). The gold boundary suite enters only at grade time via config.json test_patch; at base it is 17/17 green, at planted exactly the 5 f2p fail and all 12 p2p stay green.
env_note: No task-specific env, secrets, or network required. The per-bank categorization is deterministic and offline. Repo image is python:3.11-slim with numpy==1.26.4 / pandas==2.2.3 (plus scipy/scikit-learn/matplotlib/fuzzywuzzy/Levenshtein/flask) installed as wheels; the package is installed editable with --no-deps so the 2022-era setup.py pins (which have no cp311/arm64 wheels and fail to compile against modern numpy headers) are bypassed. The np.select string/amount logic runs unchanged on the modern pins; verified by exercising both bank categorizers directly.

defects.edge: a CREDIT whose 16-character remark is 11 zeros followed by 5 digits (exactly the layout the sibling regex ^[0]{11}[0-9]{5} matches)
defects.derivability: The length guard is pinned by the regex on the SAME line: ^[0]{11}[0-9]{5} matches exactly 11+5 = 16 characters, so the length must be 16. .eq(15) contradicts the regex; deriving it requires counting the two regex quantifiers. Nothing in any test signposts it.
defects.wrong_output: the exact-16 zero-prefixed remark is no longer labelled CHEQUE_DEPOSIT (falls through to the default TRANSFER)

defects.edge: a six-digit numeric-code CREDIT in a SAVING account (the payroll reference-code salary heuristic)
defects.derivability: A casing/spelling typo pinned by the pervasive idiom: accountType=="SAVING" occurs 45 times across the repo (and the adjacent CURRENT branch uses the same uppercase convention). "SAVINGS" never matches the value the caller passes; a grep of the account-type literal reveals the singleton.
defects.wrong_output: the six-digit savings credit is no longer labelled SALARY (subcategory stays empty)

defects.edge: a CREDIT that mirrors the immediately-preceding DEBIT (same amount and description, containing IMPS/NEFT/UPI/RTGS)
defects.change: transactions.description.shift(periods=1) -> shift(periods=2) (on the REVERSAL branch only; the type and amount shifts and the whole AUTO_PAYMENT_BOUNCE sibling keep periods=1)
defects.derivability: A structural adjacency offset. The detector compares the current row to the row immediately before it (shift 1); the near-identical AUTO_PAYMENT_BOUNCE sibling on the next line uses shift(periods=1) throughout, pinning the correct offset. With shift(2) the description no longer matches the prior row, so a genuine reversal is misfiled as AUTO_PAYMENT_BOUNCE. Requires understanding the row-adjacency pattern.
defects.wrong_output: an offsetting IMPS/NEFT/UPI/RTGS credit is labelled AUTO_PAYMENT_BOUNCE instead of REVERSAL

defects.edge: a rupee-one account-verification DEBIT expressed as the sentinel amount -1
defects.derivability: A sentinel-membership drop pinned by the pervasive isin([1, -1]) idiom used 31 times across the repo to mark the rupee-one verification/skip amounts (both the +1 credit and the -1 debit form). Dropping -1 contradicts every sibling occurrence; a grep of the idiom reveals the singleton.
defects.wrong_output: a -1 verification debit is no longer labelled ACCOUNT_VERIFICATION (falls through to UPI)

defects.edge: a BIL/INFT bill description with exactly three slashes, i.e. four segments, whose payee is the final segment
defects.derivability: A fixed-layout slice index pinned by the guard on the SAME line: str.count('/').eq(3) means four slash-separated segments (indices 0..3), so the payee is segment 3 (the last). Shifting to segment 2 grabs the numeric reference instead of the name; deriving the correct index requires reading the count guard against the layout.
defects.wrong_output: the payee name comes out as the numeric reference (which downstream cleaning then blanks), instead of the actual payee

resistance_assessment: Five single-token slips across two of ~40 bank scripts, each biting only an exact-length / sentinel / adjacency / account-type / fixed-layout edge that ordinary transactions never feed, so there is no failing local test and no run-pytest-see-red gradient (the repo ships no tests at all). A solve requires finding ALL five, which are deliberately distinct shapes (a length-eq literal, a string-casing typo, a shift-adjacency offset, a list-membership drop, a slice index) so no single pattern-match surfaces the rest. Two are easy-derivable (the SAVING->SAVINGS casing and the isin([1,-1])->[1] drop are greppable against 45 and 31 correct siblings, p~0.9); three are medium (the remark length pinned only by counting the sibling regex, the reversal adjacency pinned only by tracing the AUTO_PAYMENT_BOUNCE sibling, the payee slice pinned only by the count('/')==3 layout guard). Targeting Opus ~2-4/10.
fairness_audit: Every gold test is pure-input: it builds a real pandas DataFrame of raw transaction fields and calls the actual categorize_HDFC_transactions / categorize_ICICI_transactions, asserting on the returned category/subcategory/payee columns. There are ZERO mocks, ZERO patched attributes, and no reliance on any private helper name; a materially different but correct boundary fix (e.g. restoring len==16 vs testing the digit layout directly) still passes because the assertions pin observable output, not implementation. Each f2p is disjoint (different bank, branch and input) and each has a p2p that pins the adjacent correct behavior at both base and planted state (e.g. the +1 verification and the non-sentinel amount for D4; the 15-char and non-zero 16-char remarks for D1). All five defected branches are reachable from Controller.categorize (live code, not dead paths). Deterministic and fully offline.
